pi_items.source: task.json line 17 and task description line 5; confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 16), export_result.sh (line 16), and verifier.py
task.json description states 'The channel must listen on TCP port 6661'; confirmed in setup_task.sh line 106 and export_result.sh lines 61, 62, 69, 70
setup_task.sh line 16 creates database entry: INSERT INTO clinic_routes (facility_id, host, port) VALUES ('CLINIC_A', 'localhost', 6671); confirmed in export_result.sh line 76
setup_task.sh line 17 creates database entry: INSERT INTO clinic_routes (facility_id, host, port) VALUES ('CLINIC_B', 'localhost', 6672); confirmed in export_result.sh line 89
setup_task.sh line 16-17 database inserts; task.json description mentions 'CLINIC_A maps to port 6671'; confirmed in sample messages (setup_task.sh line 79 and 117)
setup_task.sh line 16-17 database inserts; task.json description mentions 'CLINIC_B maps to port 6672'; confirmed in sample messages (setup_task.sh line 85 and 118)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating a NextGen Connect channel named 'Dynamic_Clinic_Router' that listens on TCP port 6661. The channel uses database lookups to route HL7 messages dynamically: messages with 'CLINIC_A' in MSH-6 are routed to port 6671, and messages with 'CLINIC_B' in MSH-6 are routed to port 6672. All data is synthetic, created by the setup script with sample HL7 messages and PostgreSQL database entries containing two facility records in the 'clinic_routes' table.
